name: DeepSeek-V3-0324
archDisplay: DeepSeekMoE: 256 routed + 1 shared expert, top-8 routing, 61 layers (3 dense + 58 MoE), Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA)
note: March 2025 refresh of V3 with big reasoning/math/coding/front-end gains (AIME 39.6 to 59.4) and improved function calling and JSON output; MIT-licensed.
tpsNote: 671B MoE far exceeds a single 24GB 4090; typically deployed on multi-GPU servers or via CPU-offload dynamic GGUF quants (~150-250GB range).

results.notes: AIME 2024, up from 39.6 in Dec-2024 V3

MATH-500 pass@1 score (94.0, up from 90.2 for DeepSeek-V3), read directly from the bar chart image (figures/0324_comparison.png) embedded in the official HF model card — this fifth benchmark isn't in the card's text callouts (only MMLU-Pro/GPQA/AIME/LiveCodeBench are called out in prose) but is clearly labeled in the chart itself, which also matches the 4 already-recorded scores exactly (MMLU-Pro 81.2, GPQA Diamond 68.4, AIME 2024 59.4, LiveCodeBench 49.2).

verificationNotes: Existence and release confirmed via DeepSeek's own news post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news250325, published 2025-03-25 referencing a 3/24 model card date) and the live HF card, both stating MIT license. Note: HF safetensors index sums to ~685B raw tensor weight but DeepSeek's own papers/cards and consistent third-party reporting describe the architecture as 671B total/37B active (685B includes the multi-token-prediction head), so 671B is kept as the standard-cited figure. All 4 result rows (AIME 59.4, GPQA 68.4, MMLU-Pro 81.2, LiveCodeBench 49.2) confirmed literally on the HF card as the 'after' values of the delta callouts. Caveat (not changed in record): DeepSeek's own 'deepseek-chat' API alias now silently routes to a newer V4-Flash model (since ~Apr 2026) and deprecates 2026-07-24, so the $0.27/$1.10 figure is the original 2025 DeepSeek-hosted launch price, not what DeepSeek's API bills today; the open weights remain genuinely available via self-host or third-party providers.
